mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% signals central bank inaction, eroding real returns and causing S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPI > 2.5% periods exceed 60% positive outcomes despite the signal or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term between GDPC1 and INDPRO shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, which drive S&P 500 gains over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE < 4% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails in 60%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improved consumer confidence and construction activity, boosting equity demand and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in HOUST expansion phases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals supply chain disruptions, reducing corporate output and causing S&P 500 declines over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails in 60%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal deteriorating real demand, leading to S&P 500 losses over 60 trading days as markets discount future earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in stagflationary conditions are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200k signals strong labor market health, increasing consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in PAYEMS > 200k periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails in 60%+ of triggered instances.
